source.title: The algorithmic architecture of exploration in the human brain

confidence_rationale: Review synthesising the human-exploration literature with explicit attention to algorithmic-architectural distinctions: random vs directed exploration; structure-aware exploration that exploits correlations between options. The review also touches on the developmental dissociation between random exploration (adolescent peak) and directed exploration (adult-stable), which is the kind of empirical signature that would be expected if 314a/b/c are independent substrates. For Q-044 the review weakly supports the three-substrates outcome at the algorithmic-class level, while leaving the implementation-level dissociation open.
failure_signatures: Schulz & Gershman 2019 do not directly address the three-way distinction between novelty / uncertainty-driven curiosity / learning-progress curiosity. Their primary dichotomy is random vs directed exploration, with structure-aware exploration as a third axis. The fit to Q-044's MECH-314a/b/c carving is approximate, not exact.

As a review it inherits the caveats of its primary sources and should not be weighted too heavily to avoid double-counting evidence already captured by Daw 2006 (Q-044 sibling entry) and Wittmann 2007.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Humans use a combination of random and uncertainty-directed exploration strategies that rely on different neural systems, have different developmental trajectories, and are sensitive to different task manipulations. Recent work also identifies structure-aware exploration as a third axis: people exploit correlations and similarities between options to guide exploration.
mapping.ree_translation: The review reinforces the algorithmic-architectural framing Q-044 presupposes: exploration mechanisms are not interchangeable and should not be collapsed to a single decision-noise parameter. The structure-aware-exploration axis Schulz & Gershman identify is closely related to MECH-314b (uncertainty-driven curiosity) but with additional content about generalising across correlated states. The developmental dissociation (adolescent peak in random exploration) supports the substrate-independence outcome for AT LEAST the random/directed split.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review's primary dichotomy (random vs directed) maps onto Q-043's MECH-313/MECH-314 split, not onto Q-044's MECH-314a/b/c sub-split. For Q-044, this review provides indirect support at the algorithmic level (independent mechanisms exist) but does not directly speak to the three-way MECH-314 decomposition. Treat as supporting the GENERAL ARCHITECTURE Q-044 inherits from ARC-065 rather than as direct evidence for or against any specific Q-044 resolution outcome.
mapping.source_context: Curr Opin Neurobiol review article, synthesises 2006-2018 human exploration literature. (Reused from Q-043 lit-pull with appropriate re-framing.)

confidence_components.notes: Same source quality as the Q-043 entry. Mapping fidelity is lower here (0.65 vs 0.80 in Q-043) because the review's primary dichotomy maps onto Q-043's question, not Q-044's. Indirect support only. The 0.70 aggregate is appropriately weighted lower than the Q-043 sibling entry.
